method_atom: A collection of many interacting bosonic units arranged on a periodic array of tunnel-coupled wells, where each site supports a hierarchy of discrete internal modes (energy levels). By selectively populating a metastable higher-energy mode whose relaxation/decay time is much longer than the inter-site coupling (hopping) time, and starting from a localized, number-locked (incoherent, insulating) initial configuration, the driven system dynamically self-organizes into a macroscopically occupied, phase-coherent collective state characterized by a non-zero characteristic wavevector.
object_atom: Ultracold bosonic atoms loaded into the excited Bloch bands (higher orbitals) of a 3D optical lattice, prepared by exciting them from a lowest-band Mott-insulating state, exhibiting long-lived excited-band populations and Bose-Einstein condensation into a finite-momentum state in 1D and 2D.
brief_reason: What carries across is the metastable higher-mode occupation on a coupled-well lattice and the dynamical emergence of finite-wavevector coherence from an insulating start; what is domain-specific is the ultracold-atoms-in-optical-lattice-orbitals realization.
